chunks.text: ORDER SHEET THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA 2nd Constitution Petition No. S-435 of 2025 (Mst. Komal Aijaz and another v. P.O Sindh and others) Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on office objections ‘A’. 2. For hearing of main case. 21-11-2025 Mr. Idrees Ahmed Mangi, Advocate for petitioners. Mr. Zain-ul-Abideen, Assistant Prosecutor General. Mr. Munawar Ali Abbasi, Assistant A.G a/w SIP Ali Dost on behalf of SSP, Larkana, SIP Zulfiquar Ali on behalf of SSP, Kamber, SIP Mehran Khan of PS Market, SIP Imtiaz Ali, SHO, PS Ali Goharabad and SIP Mehboob Ali of PS Nasirabad. ************* The official respondents present today have filed their statements, which are hereby taken on record. At this stage, Mr. Mumtaz Ali Jessar, Advocate, undertakes to file Vakalatnama on behalf of private respondents No.8 to 10 and submits that due to the death of their cousin, they could not appear today. Learned counsel for the petitioners submits that petitioner No.1, being sui-juris, has contracted marriage with petitioner No.2 of her own freewill, and respondents No.8 to 10 are allegedly causing harassment to them. It is further submitted that, at the behest of said private respondents, officials of various police stations are also extending harassment; hence, the present petition has been filed seeking protection. Conversely, learned Assistant Advocate General Sindh submits that the official respondents have categorically denied the allegations of harassment and are fully committed to act strictly in accordance with law and extend due protection to the petitioners. Mr. Mumtaz Ali Jessar, learned counsel also submits that respondents No.8 to 10 neither caused any harassment to the petitioners nor do they intend to harass them in future.

Mr. Mumtaz Ali Jessar, learned counsel also submits that respondents No.8 to 10 neither caused any harassment to the petitioners nor do they intend to harass them in future. In view of the above statements, learned counsel for the petitioners seeks disposal of the petition with directions that the petitioners be provided legal protection. Accordingly, the present constitutional petition stands disposed of, with directions to the official respondents to ensure that no harassment is caused to the petitioners and that they shall act strictly in accordance with law while providing legal protection to the petitioners. The private respondents No.8 to 11 are also restrained from causing any harassment to the petitioners. Let a copy of this order be transmitted to the official respondents as well as learned A.A.G. for information and compliance. JUDGE Irshad Ali M/Steno
